title: Oven cleaner paste
subtitle: An alkaline bicarbonate and washing soda paste for baked-on oven grease
excerpt: A thick alkaline paste made from bicarbonate of soda, washing soda, and castile liquid soap for cleaning baked-on grease from oven interiors. Apply, leave overnight, and wipe away. The alkaline compounds break down the fat bonds in carbonised grease without abrasive scrubbing. Wear protective gloves.
sourceNotes: Dri-Pak technical notes on washing soda and sodium bicarbonate in cleaning applications (dri-pak.co.uk, accessed May 2026). Castile soap in alkaline cleaning formulations: Dr Bronner's use guide.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make fresh each time; the paste dries out if made ahead. Apply and leave overnight (8 hours minimum) before wiping.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within 3 days. The paste dries out and becomes harder to wipe if left longer. Make fresh for each cleaning session.

glossaryTerms.definition: Grease and carbonised food residue in ovens are acidic or fatty compounds. Strongly alkaline substances such as washing soda (sodium carbonate, pH 11 in solution) saponify fats, breaking them into water-soluble glycerol and soap molecules that can be wiped away. This is the same chemistry as soap-making, applied to oven grease.

glossaryTerms.term: Washing soda vs bicarbonate of soda
glossaryTerms.definition: Washing soda (sodium carbonate) is much more alkaline than bicarbonate of soda (sodium bicarbonate): pH 11 versus pH 8.4 in solution. Bicarbonate of soda alone is too mild to cut through heavy oven grease. Washing soda adds the chemical strength; the bicarbonate adds bulk and mild abrasion. Do not substitute one for the other in this recipe.

content.text: An oven cleaner paste works through

chemistry: the washing soda in the paste reacts with carbonised grease and breaks down the fat molecules. The bicarbonate of soda adds gentle abrasion. The castile soap helps the paste cling to vertical oven walls. Leave the paste in place overnight for the chemistry to work; wiping too soon will require more effort.

is much more alkaline than bicarbonate of soda and will irritate skin on prolonged contact. Do not use this paste on the oven's heating elements, fan casing, or self-cleaning liners. Avoid contact with aluminium surfaces, which react with strong alkalis.

items.prepNote: Provides bulk and mild abrasion. Use plain bicarbonate of soda, not baking powder.
Sodium carbonate (soda crystals), not bicarbonate. The primary alkaline degreaser. Wear gloves when handling.
Concentrated castile soap. Helps the paste adhere to oven walls.
Add a tablespoon at a time; the exact amount depends on the bicarbonate. The paste should be thick enough to spread without running.

content.text: Wearing protective gloves, combine the bicarbonate of soda and washing soda in the bowl. Add the castile liquid soap and stir. Add the distilled water a tablespoon at a time, stirring between additions, until the paste is thick and spreadable but not runny.

Remove the oven racks. Spread the paste thickly over the oven interior, avoiding the heating elements, fan, and any self-cleaning liner. Pay particular attention to the base and rear corners where grease accumulates. Leave for a minimum of 8 hours, or overnight.

Using a damp cloth, wipe away the paste and dissolved grease. Rinse the cloth repeatedly. For stubborn areas, apply a second layer of paste and leave for another few hours. Wipe all surfaces clean with a damp cloth until no paste residue remains before using the oven.

Make fresh each time. The paste will begin to dry out after 2 to 3 days in a sealed container. If any paste is left over, it can be used as a scouring paste for grill racks or hob surrounds within that time.
